rationale: The citing paper references Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) only in an introductory footnote to note that hedonic versus utilitarian is one of several labeling schemes for product types. It does not use this specific paper's acquisition-versus-forfeiture mechanism, hypotheses, or empirical findings in developing the focal theory about price discounts, bonus packs, guilt, and justification. The paper's core argument and tests are built around other cited work (for example, Wertenbroch 1998, Okada 2005), so this citation functions as background context rather than a meaningful building block.
